info.title: NetIntel API
info.description:
Network intelligence API — DNS, SSL, WHOIS, email security, web fingerprinting, OSINT, and more. 25 endpoints, all pay-per-call via x402 micropayments on Base mainnet (USDC). No API keys, no subscriptions, no rate limits.

## Payment

All endpoints (except /health) require x402 micropayment in USDC on Base mainnet (chain ID eip155:8453). Send a valid `Payment-Signature` or `X-Payment` header with each request. Payment is verified by the CDP facilitator at `https://api.cdp.coinbase.com/platform/v2/x402`. Without a valid payment header, endpoints return HTTP 402 with payment requirements in the `PAYMENT-REQUIRED` response header.

Payee wallet: `0xdaDc335482AD545296Fd7b28518A251fFCbEb9Df`

get.description: Returns server health status. Not behind paywall.
Resolves all DNS record types for a domain, parses SPF/DKIM/DMARC from TXT records, and compares A records across Google/Cloudflare/Quad9 resolvers for propagation consistency.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name to query (e.g. example.com)

get.description: Performs a TLS handshake to inspect the certificate chain, probes supported TLS versions (1.0–1.3), detects weak keys and expiry issues, and returns an overall security grade (A/B/C/F).

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain to analyze (e.g. example.com)

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: Port to connect to (default: 443)

get.description: Calculates IPv4/IPv6 subnet details from CIDR notation including network/broadcast addresses, netmask, usable host range, supernet, and possible subnets; for multiple CIDRs, detects overlaps and classifies relationships.

parameters.name: cidr
parameters.description: CIDR notation, single (10.0.1.0/24) or comma-separated for overlap detection

get.description: Follows a URL through its full redirect chain (up to 20 hops), recording status codes, timing, headers, TLS status, and protocol downgrades per hop, then returns an overall redirect health grade (A–F).

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL to trace redirects for (e.g. http://google.com)

parameters.name: max_hops
parameters.description: Maximum redirect hops to follow (default: 10, max: 20)

get.description: Fetches a URL and evaluates 10 security-critical response headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, X-XSS-Protection, CORP, COEP, COOP), detects anti-patterns, and returns an overall security grade (A–F).

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: URL or domain to audit security headers for

post.description: Validates SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records for a domain, probes multiple DKIM selectors concurrently, detects misconfigurations, and returns an overall email security grade (A–F) with transparent deductions.

domain.description: Domain to validate email authentication for

dkim_selectors.description: Optional list of DKIM selectors to probe (defaults to common selectors)

get.description: Fingerprints a domain's cloud infrastructure by probing DNS records, HTTP headers, and PTR lookups to detect CDN, WAF, hosting, DNS provider, and email provider with confidence scores and an infrastructure security grade (A–F).

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain to fingerprint (e.g. example.com)

post.description: Accepts unstructured text and a JSON Schema, then uses an LLM to extract structured data matching the schema. Supports any user-defined schema shape — contact info, invoices, product specs, medical records, legal clauses, etc. Returns the extracted object plus token usage. Fields that cannot be found are omitted or null.

raw_text.description: Unstructured text to extract data from

target_schema.description: JSON Schema defining the shape of the extracted output

extracted.description: Structured data conforming to the caller's target_schema

422.description: Model could not extract data matching the provided schema

get.description: Resolves an IP address or domain to its Autonomous System Number (ASN), network owner, country, and hosting/cloud classification — agents get structured network ownership context plus a trust score to assess infrastructure risk.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: IP address (v4 or v6) or domain name to look up

get.description: Look up domain registration metadata via RDAP — returns registrar, creation/expiry dates, nameservers, status flags, and an actionability score so agents can assess domain trustworthiness or availability risk.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name to look up (e.g. example.com)

404.description: No RDAP server found for TLD

get.description: Query the crt.sh certificate transparency log database to enumerate all SSL certificates ever issued for a domain — returns subdomains discovered, certificate issuers, validity periods, and a risk score flagging wildcard certs, expiring certs, and suspicious issuance patterns.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name to query certificate transparency logs for

parameters.name: include_subdomains
parameters.description: Include subdomain certificates (default: true)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of certificates to return (default: 100, max: 500)

get.description: Query a domain's DNS record across 10 geographically distributed public resolvers simultaneously — returns what each resolver sees, whether results are consistent, propagation percentage, and a readiness score so agents can confirm DNS changes have fully propagated globally.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name to check propagation for

parameters.name: record_type
parameters.description: DNS record type: A, AAAA, MX, TXT, CNAME, NS (default: A)

get.description: Validate a domain's DNSSEC configuration by checking for DS records at the parent zone, DNSKEY records at the domain, RRSIG signatures, and NSEC/NSEC3 records — returns a full chain-of-trust assessment and readiness score so agents can verify DNS integrity before trusting resolution results.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name to validate DNSSEC for

get.description: Check an IP address against 15 major DNS blacklists (Spamhaus, Barracuda, SORBS, etc.) simultaneously — returns which lists it appears on, a reputation score, and a threat classification so agents can make block/allow decisions instantly.

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: IPv4 address to check (e.g. 1.2.3.4)

get.description: Fetch a URL and detect the full technology stack from HTTP response headers, HTML meta tags, cookies, and script patterns — returns CMS, framework, CDN, server, analytics, and security tool detections so agents can profile any web target without a browser.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL to fingerprint (e.g. https://example.com)

get.description: Check if a password has appeared in known data breaches using the HaveIBeenPwned Pwned Passwords API with k-anonymity — the full password is never transmitted, only a 5-character SHA-1 hash prefix — returns breach count and a risk classification so agents can enforce password policies without storing or exposing credentials.

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Password to check against known data breaches

get.description: Check if a domain name is available for registration by querying RDAP and DNS — returns registration status, whether it's taken or free, expiry date if registered, and availability of the same name across 10 popular TLDs simultaneously so agents can find the best domain for a brand or project.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name with or without TLD (e.g. mybrand or mybrand.com)

get.description: Validate an email address for deliverability, detect disposable/temporary domains, identify role-based addresses, verify MX records exist, and return a trust score — so agents can qualify leads, clean lists, and gate signups without sending a single email.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address to validate (e.g. john@example.com)

get.description: Fetch any public URL and extract structured metadata — Open Graph tags, Twitter Card tags, canonical URL, title, description, favicon, article author/date, JSON-LD structured data, and content type — so agents can preview, classify, and enrich links without building their own scraper.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Public URL to extract metadata from

get.description: Fetch any article or web page and extract clean readable text stripped of navigation, ads, and boilerplate — returns the main content body, word count, estimated reading time, detected language, and key sentences so agents can read the web without a browser or third-party scraping service.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Public URL to extract content from

get.description: Parse and validate any phone number in any format, identify its country, line type (mobile/landline/VOIP/toll-free/premium), carrier region, and return all standard format variants — so agents can validate contacts, normalize phone data, and classify line types without a paid telecom API.

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: Phone number in any format (e.g. +1 555 867-5309)

parameters.name: country_hint
parameters.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code hint when no dial code present (e.g. US)

get.description: Fetch and parse a domain's robots.txt file — returns all crawl rules by user-agent, sitemap URLs, crawl delay settings, and checks whether a specific path is allowed or blocked for any bot — so agents can respect crawl policies and locate sitemaps before scraping.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name to fetch robots.txt from

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: URL path to check permission for (e.g. /api/data)

parameters.name: user_agent
parameters.description: Bot name to check rules for (default: *)

get.description: Fetch and parse any RSS 2.0 or Atom feed URL and return structured articles with title, link, description, author, publish date, and categories — so agents can monitor content sources, build news pipelines, and process any feed without building their own XML parser.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Feed URL to fetch and parse

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (1-50, default 10)

get.description: Check username availability across 20+ social platforms and developer sites simultaneously — returns which platforms have a profile registered under that handle so agents can find people, verify identities, scout brand handles, or check name availability before registration.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Username/handle to check (1-50 chars, alphanumeric + hyphens/underscores/dots)

get.description: Query the Internet Archive Wayback Machine to check if a URL has ever been archived, get the closest snapshot to a given date, retrieve the most recent capture, and return archival history stats — so agents can access historical web content, verify past content claims, and recover deleted pages.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL to check in the Wayback Machine

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Optional target date in YYYYMMDD or YYYYMMDDHHmmss format to find the closest snapshot

PaymentRequired.description: x402 payment required. Send a valid Payment-Signature or X-Payment header with USDC payment on Base mainnet.

tags.name: Security
tags.description: Security headers, IP blacklists, and breach checks

tags.name: Web
tags.description: Redirect tracing, tech fingerprinting, OG scraping, page extraction, robots.txt, RSS parsing

tags.name: OSINT
tags.description: Phone intel, username checks, Wayback Machine
